fault_hypothesis: The while-loop condition on line 56 uses `>` instead of `>=`, so when `len(order) == capacity` the loop body is never entered and no eviction occurs, violating the requirement that the cache must be strictly under capacity.

constraints.intent: After the while loop exits, the order list must be strictly under capacity; this is FALSE when len(order)==capacity because the > condition never triggers eviction.
At the end of each loop iteration the list should be strictly under capacity, confirming eviction is making progress toward the correct termination condition.
Before checking total_evicted for logging, the order list must already be strictly under capacity, which fails when the loop condition was too strict.
The returned list must be strictly under capacity per the docstring contract.
